purpose: Test whether materially new candidate architectures alter the frozen FARA/LTS-PROV minimal frontier without treating failure to find a candidate as proof of exhaustiveness.
minimum_requirement: At least one materially new architecture that is neither FARA nor LTS-PROV and was selected without optimizing against their known scores.

required: architecture proposal precedes access to candidate-specific frontier scores where practicable
proposer records conceptual source, primitives, operations, invariants, and intended reasoning commitments
candidate is evaluated under the same preservation contract and componentwise cost preorder
all adapters, decoders, bridges, canonicalizers, hidden state, and derived machinery are charged
disallowed: mere renaming or syntactic refactoring of FARA or LTS-PROV
hybrid that imports either winner wholesale without charging the imported machinery
post-hoc primitive changes made only after seeing failed obligations
selective omission of negative controls or unresolved gates

material_novelty_tests: primitive ontology differs in more than names or grouping
state and transition commitments are not commitment-equivalent to an existing candidate by a low-cost bidirectional translation
history, semantics, dependency, and revision mechanisms are independently specified
candidate has at least one discriminating architectural commitment that could change preservation or cost results
evaluation_stages: canonical mapping to the existing bounded benchmark

decision_rules.admission: A candidate enters only if every material-novelty test is resolved pass or an explicit unresolved admission is reported.
decision_rules.repair: Substantive post-freeze repair creates a new candidate version and cannot overwrite the original result.
decision_rules.frontier: The frontier is recomputed componentwise with no scalar score and no preferred-vocabulary tie breaker.
decision_rules.failure: No successful new candidate means only that this registered expansion did not alter the frontier.
decision_rules.weakest_gate: The terminal outcome cannot be stronger than the least-resolved mandatory stage.

nonclaims: failure to alter the frontier proves global minimality
the current FARA/LTS-PROV frontier is universal
actual-process correspondence follows from formal fit
